title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs · Command Brief: Systemctl Observation
summary: Diagnose a service that is enabled but unavailable by separating desired boot state, current process state, dependencies, configuration, and logs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux systemctl observation increment in a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.
Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Systemctl Observation
steps.instruction:
theory case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Systemctl Observation contract for the supplied data.

content.text: Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback. A defensible solution applies Systemctl Observation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.

content.code:
systemctl show lab-worker.service -p ActiveState -p SubState -p Result
journalctl -u lab-worker.service -b --since '10 minutes ago'
# Restart only after evidence identifies a reversible cause.
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Restarting is the first diagnostic step for every service issue. This fails the Systemctl Observation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Defend the Systemctl Observation model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.
